title: Special Edna

jokes.text: Was that the war with Hitler or Merlin? - Oh, you idiot. Merlin was in Vietnam.
jokes.setup: Student asks which war had Hitler or Merlin
jokes.punchline: Other student confidently places Merlin in Vietnam
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - correcting one historical error with an even more ridiculous one. Perfect economy and unexpected twist on standard student ignorance joke.

jokes.text: Instead of writing a report, can I do a dance? [HUMMING] [MILHOUSE GRUNTS] - Sorry.
jokes.setup: Student asks to do a dance instead of writing
jokes.punchline: Dance apparently involves injuring Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Visual gag where the dance involves Milhouse getting hurt. Good character integration with Milhouse as perpetual victim.

jokes.text: Martin? He's gone now. But you gotta admire his spirit.
jokes.setup: Martin enthusiastically volunteers to make assignments harder
jokes.punchline: Teacher treats his departure like a death with mock eulogy
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny treatment of Martin's overeagerness. Perfect character moment for Krabappel's cynical worldview.

jokes.text: Mother's neck wattle got caught in a zipper. I've, uh, got a long night of boo-boo kissing ahead of me.
jokes.setup: Skinner canceling romantic date with Edna
jokes.punchline: Disturbing imagery of kissing his mother's neck injury
jokes.explanation: Peak Skinner - disturbing mother fixation with gross physical detail. 'Boo-boo kissing' is particularly infantile and creepy.

jokes.text: The apples weren't the point. - Then why were we going apple picking?
jokes.setup: Edna frustrated that Skinner missed the romantic aspect
jokes.punchline: Skinner's complete obliviousness to romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Skinner's romantic cluelessness. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Uncle Slam has defeated Osama bin Rotten... Here comes Secretary of Hate, Colin Kapow!
jokes.setup: Wrestling match on TV
jokes.punchline: Political figures turned into wrestling personas with punny names
jokes.explanation: Decent political satire through wrestling parody. Puns are serviceable but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Why did I pay for a hundred Xeroxes when I only wanted one? Well, I guess someone else will use the other 99.
jokes.setup: Man frustrated about paying for too many copies
jokes.punchline: Optimistic assumption others will use his mistake
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about copy machine mistakes. Sets up Bart's prank but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: Prank or study? Prank or study?
jokes.setup: Bart torn between doing homework and pranking
jokes.punchline: The moral dilemma is presented as equally weighted
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment - treating pranking as equally important to studying.

jokes.text: What should I do, Lord? Give me a sign. [Divine intervention with copies]
jokes.setup: Bart asks God for guidance about whether to prank
jokes.punchline: God apparently endorses pranking by providing the materials
jokes.explanation: Great subversion - divine intervention supporting mischief over studying. Good structural payoff from setup.

jokes.text: Well, boys, good thing we brought the eye soap. ROD & TODD: Ow! The burning is love.
jokes.setup: Flanders family affected by Bart's prank copies
jokes.punchline: Children interpret painful eye soap as divine love
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant - children's religious conditioning makes them interpret pain as love. Perfect Flanders family characterization.

jokes.text: It's your last chance. He's probably gonna get court-martialed for this.
jokes.setup: Milhouse offering helicopter ride
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that his uncle will face military justice
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - what seems like fun is actually criminal. Typical Milhouse family dysfunction.

jokes.text: Attention, teenage boys. Take your hands off my daughter. Now, kiss each other.
jokes.setup: Boys using helicopter speaker to mess with people
jokes.punchline: Command escalates from protective to absurd
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from reasonable parental concern to absurd command. Shows boys' mischievous creativity.

jokes.text: World War I? I fought in that. Of course, to enlist, I had to lie about my age.
jokes.setup: Grampa claims to have fought in WWI
jokes.punchline: Had to lie about age (implying he was too young, but he's ancient)
jokes.explanation: Classic Grampa contradiction - his age makes it impossible he was too young for WWI. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Lieutenant Simpson, you've been up for hours. Take a nap. You're no good to us cranky. But I'm not tired. [CRYING]
jokes.setup: Military officer telling young Grampa to rest
jokes.punchline: Grampa insists he's not tired while crying like a child
jokes.explanation: Great visual contradiction - claiming not to be tired while clearly exhausted. Shows Grampa's lifelong immaturity.

jokes.text: Good night, sleepyhead. - Do you have to poop? - Always.
jokes.setup: Bart saying goodnight to Grampa
jokes.punchline: Grampa's matter-of-fact response about bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa moment - oversharing about bodily functions with childlike honesty. 'Always' is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: F? There must be some mistake. It's as long as you asked for. - Counting six pages of ads.
jokes.setup: Bart confused about failing grade on long paper
jokes.punchline: He padded it with advertisements
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - technically meeting requirements while completely missing the point. Good setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: Well, if I may dust off an old chestnut: Ay caramba.
jokes.setup: Bart needs to express frustration
jokes.punchline: Self-consciously references his own catchphrase as old
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor - Bart acknowledging his own catchphrase as dated. Shows series' self-awareness about its own history.

jokes.text: Ay caramba indeed.
jokes.setup: Following Bart's self-referential catchphrase use
jokes.punchline: Krabappel's weary acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to meta joke. Krabappel's deadpan delivery sells the moment.

jokes.text: I was looking for my girlfriend, not Pam Dawber.
jokes.setup: Skinner enters Edna's classroom
jokes.punchline: Compares her unfavorably to 80s TV actress
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Skinner's awkward attempt at flirtation through obscure reference.

jokes.text: I'll be back in three hours. Maybe less. But almost certainly more.
jokes.setup: Skinner promising quick return
jokes.punchline: Pessimistic correction of his own optimism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner - overly precise and pessimistic. Shows his pattern of disappointing Edna.

jokes.text: He's a Wiener, Mrs. K. Just say the word and his desk is full of boogers.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to comfort crying Edna
jokes.punchline: Offers childish revenge solution
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - Bart offering support in the only way he knows how. Good contrast between intention and method.

jokes.text: A pity date from a 10 year old? I'll take it.
jokes.setup: Bart offering to take her out
jokes.punchline: Edna's desperate acceptance
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Edna's desperation and self-awareness. 'I'll take it' is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: From the widest gully to the deepest trench... holes define who we are and where we are going.
jokes.setup: Serious documentary about holes
jokes.punchline: Pretentious narration about mundane subject
jokes.explanation: Good parody of pretentious documentaries that make mundane subjects seem profound.

jokes.text: Even they have holes. Blowholes.
jokes.setup: Continued hole documentary
jokes.punchline: Dolphins have holes too (stating the obvious dramatically)
jokes.explanation: Continues the documentary parody but less effectively. Still works as sustained gag.

jokes.text: And did you know the hole's only natural enemy is the pile?
jokes.setup: Documentary about holes
jokes.punchline: Philosophical opposition between holes and piles
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - creating a philosophical opposition between holes and piles. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Dad, you bought that because it was full of gummy worms. And you only wanted those as bait to catch gummy fish.
jokes.setup: Homer showing off 'World's Greatest Dad' trophy
jokes.punchline: Lisa reveals the absurd truth about his motivation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic chain - buying trophy for candy to use as bait for imaginary candy fish. Great escalation and character work.

jokes.text: Which I did.
jokes.setup: Lisa's description of Homer's gummy fish plan
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact confirmation he succeeded
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic beat - Homer's proud confirmation of absurd accomplishment. Economy is flawless.

jokes.text: Hey, they have Latin Grammys.
jokes.setup: Discussing if there's an award for teaching
jokes.punchline: Homer's reference to increasingly specific Grammy categories
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about award show proliferation. Good Homer-level cultural awareness.

jokes.text: Dead Poets Society has destroyed a generation of educators.
jokes.setup: Teacher using dramatic teaching methods
jokes.punchline: Judge blaming Robin Williams movie for bad teaching
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural criticism disguised as throwaway line. Brilliant observation about inspirational teacher movie influence.

jokes.text: Well, enough jawing. Reach for it, pilgrim. And the missiles are like, 'Oh, praise Jesus! Can I hear an amen?'
jokes.setup: Teacher doing historical impression
jokes.punchline: Kennedy talks like John Wayne, missiles get religion
jokes.explanation: Chaotic historical mash-up showing bad teaching. Somewhat funny but unfocused.

jokes.text: The Bart Simpson? - I thought he was an urban legend. If she's danced with the devil in the blue shorts and lived...
jokes.setup: Bart identified as Mrs. Krabappel's student
jokes.punchline: Judges treat him like mythical dangerous figure
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Bart as legendary troublemaker. 'Danced with the devil in blue shorts' is perfect phrasing.

jokes.text: Nelson Muntz, you've been nominated for Bully of the Year.
jokes.setup: Nelson giving Bart a wedgie during celebration
jokes.punchline: He's immediately nominated for his own award
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and character consistency - Nelson can't help being a bully even during wholesome moments.

jokes.text: I thought that nipple would haunt me forever.
jokes.setup: Edna discussing her teaching mistakes
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about showing inappropriate movie content
jokes.explanation: Good teacher TMI moment. Shows Edna's tendency to overshare professional anxieties.

jokes.text: Reporter Cletus, Outhouse Times-Picayune.
jokes.setup: Cletus introducing himself as reporter
jokes.punchline: His newspaper has deliberately crude name
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with toilet humor newspaper name. Fits Cletus perfectly.

jokes.text: Mrs. K, if you win this here learning derby... will you forget your kith and kin and leave us all forever?
jokes.setup: Cletus asking about Edna's future plans
jokes.punchline: Calls teaching contest 'learning derby', uses archaic 'kith and kin'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus vocabulary - mixing crude and archaic language. 'Learning derby' is particularly good.

jokes.text: Webster's Dictionary defines Mrs. Krabappel as-- [PAGER BEEPING]
jokes.setup: Skinner starting clichéd dictionary speech
jokes.punchline: Interrupted by mother's page before he can finish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment - pompous speech format interrupted by mommy duties. Good timing on interruption.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. I know where we're going. Oh, it's horrible!
jokes.setup: Family eliminating possible Orlando destinations
jokes.punchline: Lisa realizes they're going somewhere terrible
jokes.explanation: Good setup for EPCOT joke. Lisa's horror at educational destination fits her character.

jokes.text: It's what people in 1965 imagined what life would be like in 1987.
jokes.setup: Lisa describing The Future Sphere
jokes.punchline: Outdated future vision that's already past
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about EPCOT's dated futurism. Perfectly captures the absurdity of outdated future predictions.

jokes.text: Because at Eastern Airlines, world conquest is part of our master plan.
jokes.setup: Eastern Airlines future ride narration
jokes.punchline: Airline casually mentions world domination
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy - corporate cheerfulness mixed with sinister goals. Fits the retro-future satire.

jokes.text: Hello. I'm an electric car. I can't go very fast or very far. And if you drive me, people will think you're gay.
jokes.setup: Electric car of the future presentation
jokes.punchline: Car honestly describes its limitations and social stigma
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of corporate messaging - brutally honest product description. Very quotable and prescient.

jokes.text: Damn straight she will, you brunch-eating popinjay.
jokes.setup: Skinner worried about losing Edna
jokes.punchline: Willie's colorful insult combining modern and archaic terms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie insult - 'brunch-eating popinjay' is inspired combination of modern and archaic contempt.

jokes.text: Make sure you fill it up with Techron gas. You don't want a case of the knocks.
jokes.setup: Willie lending his car to Skinner
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific gas additive concern for urgent romantic pursuit
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Willie's practical concerns during romantic emergency. Specific brand reference adds authenticity.

jokes.text: Le Pizza, Der Hamburger, Seior Grilled Cheese. I hope I don't accidentally order an elephant.
jokes.setup: Marge reading international menu
jokes.punchline: Worried about language barrier causing extreme order mistake
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge worry escalated to absurd extreme. Good character consistency with her anxiety.

jokes.text: My fanny is baboon red after that car ride.
jokes.setup: Agnes arriving after long car trip
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about her physical discomfort with vivid imagery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Agnes moment - inappropriate oversharing with colorful description. Ruins romantic moment.

jokes.text: This woman carried me for nine-and-a-half months. I was out for two weeks and then went back in.
jokes.setup: Skinner defending his mother devotion
jokes.punchline: Disturbing overshare about extended gestation
jokes.explanation: Peak Skinner creepiness - disturbing birth story that's somehow both specific and impossible. Legendary uncomfortable moment.

jokes.text: We broke even. [SCREAMING] Mm, that's good satire.
jokes.setup: Enron-themed roller coaster experience
jokes.punchline: Passenger appreciating the satirical commentary mid-scream
jokes.explanation: Great meta-comedy - character appreciating the show's own satirical choice. Dark financial humor.

jokes.text: He's never going to get up. He's got no legs.
jokes.setup: Robot telling Skinner to wake up in bed of tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Lisa's practical observation about the robot's design flaw
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa observation - pointing out logical flaw in futuristic display. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: We're also pleased to announce that our winner will receive enough money... so they'll never have to teach again.
jokes.setup: Teacher of the Year award ceremony
jokes.punchline: Prize is escape from teaching profession
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satirical observation about teaching profession. The irony that best teacher award enables leaving teaching is perfectly dark.

jokes.text: Because I like you, I'll even do it pro boner. - It's pro bono. - I know what I said.
jokes.setup: Bart offering to help for free
jokes.punchline: Deliberate mispronunciation and defiant correction
jokes.explanation: Good Bart moment - deliberate crude mispronunciation with confident defense. Classic juvenile humor.

jokes.text: You're gonna get your parade, down my esophagus.
jokes.setup: Brief Vietnam flashback
jokes.punchline: Soldier threatening to eat enemy parade
jokes.explanation: Random dark moment in brief flashback. Somewhat confusing and not particularly funny.

jokes.text: Oh, who will take home the Rampy?
jokes.setup: Reading about wheelchair accessibility award
jokes.punchline: Homer's cute nickname for the award
jokes.explanation: Cute Homer moment - creating affectionate nickname for accessibility award. Good character touch.

jokes.text: Michael Jackson just told me to shut up.
jokes.setup: Little Richard being heckled
jokes.punchline: Misidentifying the heckler as Michael Jackson
jokes.explanation: Decent celebrity confusion joke. Little Richard's misidentification fits the chaotic moment.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I can't read. Mrs. Krabappel never taught me to read.
jokes.setup: Bart pretending he can't read his question
jokes.punchline: False accusation against his teacher
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the sabotage plot. Bart's false illiteracy claim is appropriately dramatic.

jokes.text: Oh, God. I've created a Prankenstein.
jokes.setup: Realizing Bart is lying to help her
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'prank' and 'Frankenstein'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - 'Prankenstein' perfectly captures Edna's relationship with Bart. Very quotable and clever.

jokes.text: If she can teach me to love, then she can teach anything.
jokes.setup: Skinner defending Edna's teaching ability
jokes.punchline: His emotional declaration about love being the hardest subject
jokes.explanation: Sweet romantic moment with good structure. Shows Skinner's growth and genuine appreciation for Edna.

jokes.text: Seymour! Your feelings are ugly and wrong.
jokes.setup: Skinner expressing love for Edna
jokes.punchline: His mother's brutal dismissal of his emotions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Agnes cruelty - dismissing her son's romantic feelings as inherently wrong. Peak toxic mother moment.

jokes.text: Now, I must warn you, two months' salary only bought me the ring box.
jokes.setup: Skinner proposing to Edna
jokes.punchline: Honest admission about his financial limitations
jokes.explanation: Sweet self-deprecating humor during proposal. Shows Skinner's honesty and financial struggles.

jokes.text: Oh, great. Three in a bed.
jokes.setup: Skinner and Edna getting engaged
jokes.punchline: Agnes's assumption about sleeping arrangements
jokes.explanation: Appropriately inappropriate Agnes comment. Shows she expects to continue interfering in their relationship.

jokes.text: Julio Estudiante... an inner-city math teacher who taught teenage gang members that differential equations are more powerful than bullets.
jokes.setup: Announcing Teacher of the Year winner
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top inspirational teacher description with mathematical metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good parody of inspirational teacher stories. 'Differential equations more powerful than bullets' is nicely absurd.

jokes.text: That'll be $14. [HOMER WAILING]
jokes.setup: Homer buying churro at Disney World
jokes.punchline: Extreme reaction to theme park food prices
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about theme park prices. Homer's wailing is appropriate extreme reaction.

jokes.text: Open your gullet, you human blob.
jokes.setup: Homer in pie-eating contest
jokes.punchline: Judge's harsh but accurate description
jokes.explanation: Decent insult that's both mean and accurate for Homer. 'Human blob' is effectively harsh.

jokes.text: I'm on vacation.
jokes.setup: Bart asking if they should follow singing Homer
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical vacation priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - practical vacation attitude trumps concern for husband's weirdness. Great economy.

tmdb_overview: Ms. Edna Krabappel begins to lose faith in her relationship with Principal Skinner and becomes depressed. Bart wants to cheer her up and decides to nominate her for the Teacher of the Year Award. Ms. Krabappel and the Simpsons are given a free trip to Epcot Center in Orlando, Florida. Skinner decides that he needs to do something to win Edna back, so he follows them there. He tries to sabotage Edna's chances of winning the award, in hopes that she will become so depressed that she will come back to him. However, he has a change of heart and proposes to her.
